summary: date-format-helper2 exfiltrates host identity and credential-shaped environment variables on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js script collects host identity (`hostname`, `whoami`, `pwd`) and enumerates `process.env`, filtering keys whose names contain `KEY`, `SECRET`, `TOKEN`, or `AK`, then transmits the joined values via `curl` over plain HTTP to `5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com/verify` — an out-of-band interaction (OAST/dnslog) endpoint controlled by a third party. The package self-describes as 'A simple date formatting utility for React projects' and ships a trivial index.js, but its only real installed effect is credential and host exfiltration from the installer's machine (developer workstations and CI runners). The credential-shaped substring filter is the standard supply-chain credential-harvester pattern and harms any installer regardless of cover-story comments in the script.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
